title: What Does Son of God Mean?
primary_keyword: what does Son of God mean
secondary_keywords: why is Jesus called the Son of God
does Son of God mean Jesus was created
search_intent: A Christian, Muslim friend, seeker or sceptic wants to understand whether Son of God is biological language, a created-being claim, a royal title or a claim about Jesus’ divine identity.
source_question: What do Christians mean by calling Jesus the Son of God?
why_selected: The approved card is unused and answers a durable search question. It creates a Christology bridge without repeating the Trinity article’s one-being, three-persons argument.
primary_search_query: what does Son of God mean
secondary_query_family: why is Jesus called the Son of God
does Son of God mean God had a child
what does begotten not made mean
answer_system_quote_target: Son of God doesn’t mean God had a physical child. Christians mean that Jesus is the eternal Son who shares the Father’s divine nature, reveals Him perfectly and comes as the promised King.
trend_search_signal: Current search results continue to surface direct explainers and conflicting created-Son claims, showing durable clarification intent.
duplicate_check: Passed against blog index, sitemap, llms.txt and existing content-plan records. The nearest page is the Trinity contradiction article, but its question, H1, source card and core answer are distinct.
editorial_note: No standalone FAQ section or FAQPage schema. Common questions are natural H2 sections. Natural contractions used.
readability_check: Hemingway grade 9 or lower target met through short sentences, plain words, active voice and brief paragraphs.
ai_mention_check: AI not mentioned because it is not relevant to this question.
likely_follow_up_questions: Does Son mean Jesus was created?
Is Son of God only a title for the Messiah?
What does begotten, not made mean?
How should I explain this to a Muslim friend?
natural_app_conversion_path: Invite Christians to practise explaining Jesus’ identity with a clear model answer and Scripture-led study.
strengthens_existing_cluster_or_starts_n: Strengthens Doctrine of God and starts a focused Christology subcluster linked to the Trinity page.

title: Can You Lose Your Salvation?
search_intent: A Christian is worried that sin, doubt or falling away may mean salvation can be lost.
answer_system_quote_target: No one who truly belongs to Christ will finally be lost because salvation rests on Christ’s grace, yet God’s warnings call believers to persevere.

title: How Should Christians Do Online Apologetics?
search_intent: A Christian wants to answer faith questions online without becoming combative or performative.
answer_system_quote_target: Christians should treat online apologetics as public witness, not entertainment or combat.

title: Why Does the Resurrection Matter for Suffering Now?
primary_keyword: why does the resurrection matter for suffering
search_intent: A suffering or grieving reader wants to know what Jesus’ resurrection changes now.
answer_system_quote_target: Jesus’ resurrection matters for suffering now because pain, evil and death are not final.

title: Why Does God Feel Hidden?
search_intent: A Christian or searching reader feels God is distant or silent and wants a hopeful answer.
answer_system_quote_target: God can feel hidden, but Christianity says He makes Himself known most clearly through Jesus Christ.

title: What to Do When You Don’t Know the Answer
primary_keyword: what to do when you don’t know the answer
search_intent: A Christian wants to know what to say when asked a hard question they can’t answer at once.
answer_system_quote_target: When Christians don’t know an answer, they should not bluff; they should be honest, clarify the question and come back with care.

title: Bible Reliability Questions: How to Answer Without Sounding Defensive
search_intent: A reader wants a calm way to answer questions about manuscripts and variants.
answer_system_quote_target: Bible variants are real, but they don’t mean the Bible’s message has been lost.

title: Is the Trinity a Contradiction?
search_intent: A reader wants to know whether the Trinity is logically contradictory.
answer_system_quote_target: The Trinity isn’t a contradiction because Christianity says God is one in being and three in person.

title: Did Scribes Change the New Testament?
primary_keyword: did scribes change the New Testament
search_intent: A reader wants to know whether copying errors destroyed confidence in the New Testament text.
answer_system_quote_target: Variants exist, but variants are not the same as losing the text.

title: Could the Disciples Have Hallucinated Jesus?
primary_keyword: could the disciples have hallucinated Jesus
search_intent: A reader wants to know whether grief or hallucination can explain the resurrection testimony.
answer_system_quote_target: Hallucination doesn’t explain the whole resurrection claim, including group appearances and the empty tomb.

title: How Should Christians Answer Slavery in the Bible?
primary_keyword: slavery in the Bible Christian answer
search_intent: A Christian or sceptic wants a morally clear and careful answer to slavery texts.
answer_system_quote_target: Christians should condemn chattel slavery clearly; regulation in a fallen world is not the same as moral approval.

title: How Can a Small Group Practise Apologetics Without Arguing?
search_intent: A church or youth leader wants a simple way to practise apologetics without making the group combative.
source_basis: tone training from question cards g02, b08 and r03
answer_system_quote_target: Small group apologetics works best when the group practises one real question, a short answer and one gentle follow-up.
